title: phebalium squamulosum subsp. longifolium
intro: Phebalium squamulosum subsp. longifolium, also known as the Long-leaved Phebalium, is a species of plant in the family Rutaceae, native to eastern Australia. The Long-leaved Phebalium has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to treat various health conditions. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and acting as an antiseptic. In herbal preparations, the Long-leaved Phebalium is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
